general.title: General
general.language_help: Changes take effect immediately. Some system prompts remain in English.
general.conductor_profile_description: Tell us a little about yourself so that agents created under Maestro know how to work and communicate with you. As the conductor, you orchestrate the symphony of AI agents. (Optional, max 1000 characters)
general.conductor_profile_placeholder: e.g., I'm a senior developer working on a React/TypeScript project. I prefer concise explanations and clean code patterns...
general.shell_description: Choose which shell to use for terminal sessions. Select any shell and configure a custom path if needed.
general.shell_custom_path_help: Override the auto-detected shell path. Leave empty to use the detected path.
general.shell_args_help: Additional CLI arguments passed to every shell session (e.g., --login, -c).
general.env_vars_description: apply to all terminal sessions and AI agent processes. Format: KEY=VALUE (one per line). Variables with special characters should be quoted. Agent-specific settings can override these values. Typical use cases: API keys, proxy settings, custom tool paths.
general.env_vars_tooltip_agents: All AI agent processes (Claude, OpenCode, etc.)
general.env_vars_tooltip_override: Agent-specific settings can override these values.
general.env_vars_tooltip_full: Environment variables configured here are available to all terminal sessions, all AI agent processes (Claude, OpenCode, etc.), and any spawned child processes. Agent-specific settings can override these values.
general.log_level_help: Higher levels show fewer logs. Debug shows all logs, Error shows only errors.
general.gh_path_help: Specify the full path to the <1>gh</1> binary if it's not in your PATH. Used for Auto Run worktree features.
general.input_behavior_description: Configure how to send messages in each mode. Choose between Enter or {{metaKey}}+Enter for each input type.
general.input_enter_to_send: Press Enter to send. Use Shift+Enter for new line.
general.input_meta_to_send: Press {{metaKey}}+Enter to send. Enter creates new line.
general.history_toggle_title: Enable "History" by default for new tabs
general.history_toggle_description: When enabled, new AI tabs will have the "History" toggle on by default, saving a synopsis after each completion
general.thinking_mode_title: Show AI thinking/reasoning content for new tabs
general.thinking_mode_off: Thinking hidden, only final responses shown
general.thinking_mode_on: Thinking streams live, clears on completion
general.thinking_mode_sticky: Thinking streams live and stays visible
general.tab_naming_title: Automatically name tabs based on first message
general.tab_naming_description: When you send your first message to a new tab, an AI will analyze it and generate a descriptive tab name. The naming request runs in parallel and leaves no history.
general.auto_scroll_description: Automatically scroll to the bottom when new AI output arrives. When disabled, a floating button appears for new messages.
general.power_prevent_sleep_description: Keeps your computer awake when AI agents are busy or Auto Run is active
general.power_linux_note: Note: May have limited support on some Linux desktop environments.
general.rendering_gpu_description: Use software rendering instead of GPU. Requires restart to take effect.
general.rendering_confetti_description: Skip celebratory confetti effects on achievements and milestones
general.updates_description: Automatically check for new Maestro versions when the app starts
general.beta_title: Include beta and release candidate updates
general.beta_description: Opt-in to receive pre-release versions (e.g., v0.11.1-rc, v0.12.0-beta). These may contain experimental features and bugs.
general.privacy_description: Help improve Maestro by automatically sending crash reports. No personal data is collected. Changes take effect after restart.
general.stats_enable_description: Track queries and Auto Run sessions for the dashboard.
general.stats_time_range_help: Time range shown when opening the Usage Dashboard.
general.stats_clear_help: Remove old query events, Auto Run sessions, and tasks from the stats database.
general.wakatime_description: Track coding activity in Maestro sessions via WakaTime.
general.wakatime_cli_installing: WakaTime CLI is being installed automatically...
general.wakatime_detailed_description: Track per-file write activity. Sends file paths (not content) to WakaTime.
general.wakatime_api_key_help: Get your API key from wakatime.com/settings/api-key. Keys are stored locally in ~/.maestro/settings.json.
general.storage_description: Choose where Maestro stores settings, sessions, and groups (including global environment variables, agents, and configurations). Use a synced folder (iCloud Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive) to share across devices.
general.storage_sync_note: Note: Only run Maestro on one device at a time to avoid sync conflicts.
general.storage_restart_required: Restart Maestro for changes to take effect

display.log_buffer_help: Maximum number of log messages to keep in memory. Older logs are automatically removed.
display.output_lines_help: Long outputs will be collapsed into a scrollable window. Set to "All" to always show full output.
display.alignment_help: Position your messages on the left or right side of the chat. AI responses appear on the opposite side.
display.native_title_bar_description: Use the OS native title bar instead of Maestro's custom title bar. Requires restart.
display.auto_hide_menu_bar_description: Hide the application menu bar. Press Alt to toggle visibility. Applies to Windows and Linux. Requires restart.
display.document_graph_show_external_description: Display external website links as nodes. Can be toggled in the graph view.
display.document_graph_max_nodes_help: Limits initial graph size for performance. Use "Load more" to show additional nodes.
display.context_warnings_description: Display warning banners when context window usage reaches configurable thresholds
display.ignore_patterns_description: Configure glob patterns for folders to exclude when indexing local files in the file explorer. Excluding large directories (like .git) reduces memory usage and speeds up file tree loading.

shortcuts.no_agents_note: Note: Most functionality is unavailable until you've created your first agent.
shortcuts.help_text_prefix: Not all shortcuts can be modified. Press
shortcuts.help_text_suffix: from the main interface to view the full list of keyboard shortcuts.

llm.test_help: Test sends a simple prompt to verify connectivity and configuration
llm.ollama_api_error: Ollama API error: {{status}}. Make sure Ollama is running locally.

env_editor.label: Environment Variables (optional)
env_editor.description: Environment variables passed to all terminal sessions and AI agent processes.
env_editor.invalid_name: Invalid variable name: only letters, numbers, and underscores allowed and must not start with a number.
env_editor.invalid_value: Invalid value: contains disallowed special characters; quote or escape them if you intend to include them.

ignore_patterns.honor_gitignore_description: When enabled, patterns from .gitignore files will also be excluded from indexing.
ignore_patterns.no_patterns: No ignore patterns configured. All folders will be indexed.

ssh_remote_ignore.title: Remote Ignore Patterns
ssh_remote_ignore.description: Configure glob patterns for folders to exclude when indexing remote files via SSH. These patterns apply to all SSH connections.

ssh_remotes.title: SSH Remote Hosts
ssh_remotes.description: Configure remote hosts where AI agents can be executed via SSH. This allows running agents on powerful remote machines or servers with specific tools installed.
ssh_remotes.no_remotes_hint: Add a remote host to run AI agents on external machines

ssh_modal.display_name_help: A friendly name to identify this remote configuration
ssh_modal.host_placeholder: hostname, IP, or SSH config alias
ssh_modal.host_help: Hostname, IP address, or Host pattern from ~/.ssh/config
ssh_modal.username_help: Leave empty to use SSH config or system defaults
ssh_modal.private_key_help: Leave empty to use SSH config or ssh-agent
ssh_modal.env_vars_help: Environment variables passed to agents running on this remote host
ssh_modal.disabled_description: Disabled remotes won't be available for selection

agent_config.remote_path_help: Remote command/binary for {{binaryName}}. Leave empty to use default.
agent_config.local_path_help: Path to the {{binaryName}} binary. Edit to override the auto-detected path.
agent_config.custom_args_help: Additional CLI arguments appended to all calls to this agent
agent_config.env_vars_help: Agent-specific environment variables (overrides global environment variables from Settings). These are passed to all calls to this agent.

agent_selector.no_agents: No AI agents detected. Please install Claude Code or another supported agent.

ssh_selector.label: SSH Remote Execution
ssh_selector.help: Execute this agent on a remote host via SSH instead of locally
ssh_selector.configure_hint: Configure remotes in Settings → SSH Remotes.

encore.title: Encore Features
encore.description: Optional features that extend Maestro's capabilities. Enable the ones you want. Disabled features are completely hidden from shortcuts, menus, and the command palette. Contributors building new features should consider gating them here to keep the core experience focused.

director_notes.title: Director's Notes
director_notes.description: Unified history view and AI-generated synopsis across all sessions
director_notes.no_agents: No agents available. Please install Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, or Factory Droid.
director_notes.provider_help: The AI agent used to generate synopsis summaries
director_notes.lookback_help: How far back to look when generating notes (can be adjusted per-report)
